Looking at the mockup for inventarios.html (GAP-ALM-003 · Sistema de Kitting por Folio), there are 6 screens to build, starting with the Dashboard General.

The screens cover the full workflow from picking materials by folio through to traceability tracking. I'll need to create the type definitions, seed data ported from the HTML mockup, constants for status colors and helpers, and a store with context and actions. Then I'll set up the routes under inventarios with a layout that initializes the store, plus individual pages for each screen. I also need to update the app-sidebar to add the Inventarios group with its navigation links.

For the design system, I'm sticking strictly to DS components—Card, Table, Select, Dialog, Badge, Button, Progress, Alert, Separator, Empty, StatCard, StatusBadge, and toast from Sonner. The dashboard needs KPIs, recent folios, linekeeper notifications, and celda progress. Instead of custom SVG progress rings, I'll use the linear Progress component from the DS to keep everything consistent. For the picking, carritos, confirmación, and linekeeper screens, I need working interactions—selecting a folio triggers the relevant list, marking items as surtido updates state, and actions like printing or confirming bags show toasts or dialogs. The trazabilidad screen will have a filterable history table with export capability.

Now I'm modeling the data structure. Each folio has a number, VIN, unit, level, responsible person, date, status (urgente/proceso/completado), start time, and an array of celdas. Each celda has a letter, icon, name, and materials. Materials track part number, description, quantity, unit of measure, and a surtido flag for whether they're ready. For confirmación, I'll add a separate confirmada flag so picking and carrito use surtido while confirmation tracks its own state. Notifications in linekeeper will have an ID, folio reference, unit, VIN, and type.

For trazabilidad entries, I'm tracking folio, unit, celda, material, quantity, date, time, responsible person, and whether it's a Kit or Aditiva type. The celda icons map from the HTML (shield, window, bolt, circle) to lucide equivalents. I'll store the icon-to-letter mapping in constants to keep it serializable and avoid storing functions in localStorage. Now I'm building out the types, store, seed data, and routes as a self-contained module like compras.

<script lang="ts">
	import { useComprasStore } from '$lib/tps/compras/store.svelte.js';
	import { PACKAGE_STATUS_COLORS, CENTROS_COSTO, fmtMoney } from '$lib/tps/compras/constants.js';
	import type { PackageStatus } from '$lib/tps/compras/types.js';
	import StatusBadge from '$lib/tps/components/status-badge.svelte';
	import * as Card from '$lib/components/ui/card/index.js';
	import * as Table from '$lib/components/ui/table/index.js';
	import * as Select from '$lib/components/ui/select/index.js';
	import * as Dialog from '$lib/components/ui/dialog/index.js';
	import * as Alert from '$lib/components/ui/alert/index.js';
	import { Badge } from '$lib/components/ui/badge/index.js';
	import { Button } from '$lib/components/ui/button/index.js';
	import { Textarea } from '$lib/components/ui/textarea/index.js';
	import { toast } from 'svelte-sonner';
	import Info from '@lucide/svelte/icons/info';
	import RotateCcw from '@lucide/svelte/icons/rotate-ccw';
	import Check from '@lucide/svelte/icons/check';
	import Ban from '@lucide/svelte/icons/ban';
	import PlusCircle from '@lucide/svelte/icons/circle-plus';
	import CircleCheckBig from '@lucide/svelte/icons/circle-check-big';
	import CircleX from '@lucide/svelte/icons/circle-x';

	const store = useComprasStore();
	const data = $derived(store.state);

	let ccFilter = $state<string>('todos');
	let estadoFilter = $state<PackageStatus | 'todos'>('todos');
	let nivelFilter = $state<string>('todos');

	const filtered = $derived.by(() => {
		let items = data.paquetes;
		if (ccFilter !== 'todos') items = items.filter((p) => p.centroCosto === ccFilter);
		if (estadoFilter !== 'todos') items = items.filter((p) => p.estado === estadoFilter);
		if (nivelFilter !== 'todos') items = items.filter((p) => p.nivel === +nivelFilter);
		return items;
	});

	const ccLabel = $derived(ccFilter === 'todos' ? 'Todos los centros de costo' : `${ccFilter} · ${CENTROS_COSTO.find((c) => c.id === ccFilter)?.nombre}`);
	const estadoLabel = $derived(estadoFilter === 'todos' ? 'Todos los estados' : estadoFilter);
	const nivelLabel = $derived(nivelFilter === 'todos' ? 'Todos los niveles' : `Nivel ${nivelFilter}`);

	function resetFilters() {
		ccFilter = 'todos';
		estadoFilter = 'todos';
		nivelFilter = 'todos';
	}

	// --- Detalle ---
	let detailId = $state<string | null>(null);
	const detail = $derived(detailId ? store.getPaquete(detailId) : undefined);

	// --- Autorizar / Rechazar ---
	let authId = $state<string | null>(null);
	let authMode = $state<'autorizar' | 'rechazar'>('autorizar');
	let comment = $state('');
	const authPkg = $derived(authId ? store.getPaquete(authId) : undefined);

	function openAuth(id: string, mode: 'autorizar' | 'rechazar') {
		authId = id;
		authMode = mode;
		comment = '';
		detailId = null;
	}

	function confirmAuth() {
		if (!authPkg) return;
		if (authMode === 'autorizar') {
			store.authorizePaquete(authPkg.id, comment);
			toast.success('Paquete autorizado correctamente.');
		} else {
			store.rejectPaquete(authPkg.id, comment);
			toast.error('Paquete rechazado. Regresa al flujo.');
		}
		authId = null;
	}

	const bitacoraIcon = { creacion: PlusCircle, aprobado: CircleCheckBig, rechazado: CircleX };
	const bitacoraColor = { creacion: 'text-muted-foreground', aprobado: 'text-emerald-500', rechazado: 'text-destructive' };
</script>
